cards.title: The Unexpected Return
cards.art_description: Tessa opens her bedroom door to find Cole Stone, her childhood tormentor, sitting casually on her bed. He's tall, gorgeous, with ocean-blue eyes and his signature smirk. He's back from military school.
cards.flavor_text: "Hello, Shortcake."
cards.scene_context: Cole's return from military school turns Tessa's life upside down. He's no longer just the boy who bullied her—he's a completely different kind of danger.

cards.title: The Kit Kat Thief
cards.art_description: Cole lounging in Tessa's room, casually unwrapping a Kit Kat from her supposedly top-secret hidden stash. Tessa stands with hands on her hips, glaring at him in frustration.
cards.flavor_text: "You know these are terrible for you, Shortcake."
cards.scene_context: Cole's constant invasion of Tessa's space and theft of her Kit Kats are infuriating, but they establish an undeniable intimacy. He knows all her hiding spots, and all her secrets.

cards.title: The Underwear Drawer
cards.art_description: Cole holding up a ridiculous piece of Tessa's underwear with a teasing grin. Tessa's face is bright red as she leaps to snatch it out of his hand. Chaos in her bedroom.
cards.flavor_text: "Privacy is a privilege, not a right."
cards.scene_context: Cole's shameless habit of going through her underwear drawer is designed entirely to fluster her. It breaks down the walls of polite distance between them with pure, unfiltered mischief.

cards.title: The Pool Table Confrontation
cards.art_description: A crowded, dimly lit high school basement party. Cole stands menacingly in front of a green felt pool table, stepping between a visibly flustered Tessa and a drunken guy trying to hit on her. Cole's jaw is clenched, eyes furious, radiating dangerous protective energy.
cards.flavor_text: "Don't strip on top of the pool table, Shortcake."
cards.scene_context: The infamous Chapter 17 party scene. Cole's intense, borderline-feral protectiveness over Tessa is on full display. It's chaotic, tension-filled, and undeniably proves he considers her strictly off-limits to everyone else.

cards.title: One Punch for Her
cards.art_description: Cole's fist connects with his own brother Jay's jaw. Jay reels backward in shock. Cole's expression is pure, protective fury. Tessa watches, stunned.
cards.flavor_text: "You broke her heart. I broke your jaw."
cards.scene_context: Tessa has loved Jay for years, but when Jay betrays her, Cole doesn't hesitate to violently defend her honor—even against his own brother.

cards.title: Cold Stone
cards.art_description: Cole and Tessa sharing ice cream at night. He looks at her with ocean-blue eyes, completely stripped of his usual sarcasm and bravado. He gently wipes a smudge of ice cream off her cheek. Intimate, vulnerable, portrait 5:7.
cards.flavor_text: "I'm terrified of how much I want you."
cards.scene_context: The bad boy finally lets his guard down. Underneath the teasing, the Kit Kat theft, and the arrogance is a boy who is deeply, hopelessly in love with his Shortcake.

cards.title: The Motorcycle Escape
cards.art_description: Tessa clinging tightly to Cole as they speed down an empty highway on his motorcycle at sunset. Her eyes are closed, a genuine smile on her face. Cole looks back at her in the rearview mirror. Wind in their hair, warm golden hour light.
cards.flavor_text: "Hold on tight."
cards.scene_context: Riding Cole's motorcycle represents Tessa finally letting go of her insecurities and fears. With him, she finds the freedom to be bold, reckless, and truly happy.

cards.title: The Cafe Angel
cards.art_description: Do not alter any details in the original image. Only extend the background naturally to fill a 6:5 landscape aspect ratio (1800×1500px minimum), matching the existing style, lighting, and color palette seamlessly.
cards.flavor_text: Angel wings on the wall. A real one in front of him.
cards.scene_context: A chill afternoon at Artjuna cafe in Goa with their dance crew friends. After hanging out at the bohemian cafe, they played table tennis together for the first time in ages — competitive, laughing, and completely in their element.

cards.title: The Hills She Climbed
cards.art_description: Do not alter any details in the original image. Only extend the background naturally to fill a 6:5 landscape aspect ratio (1800×1500px minimum), matching the existing style, lighting, and color palette seamlessly.
cards.flavor_text: Clouds behind them. Mountains beneath them. Magic between them.
cards.scene_context: At the tea estates of Munnar with friends. He had to convince her endlessly to climb just a few steps up a hill for a photo together — and it came out beautiful. She struck one of their signature poses, hands up in excitement, and he found her absolutely adorable.

cards.title: Above the City
cards.art_description: Do not alter any details in the original image. Only extend the background naturally to fill a 6:5 landscape aspect ratio (1800×1500px minimum), matching the existing style, lighting, and color palette seamlessly.
cards.flavor_text: Stunning view. Nothing compared to her.
cards.scene_context: An anniversary date at Skyye Lounge, a high rooftop restaurant with a stunning view, perfect breeze, and beautiful lighting. After much convincing, they took some pictures together — and they came out so well that it became their go-to photo to show family.

cards.title: The Steamy Girl
cards.art_description: Do not alter any details in the original image. Only extend the background naturally to fill a 6:5 landscape aspect ratio (1800×1500px minimum), matching the existing style, lighting, and color palette seamlessly.
cards.flavor_text: She missed the pool. The pool missed out.
cards.scene_context: On a Goa trip with their college crew, she fell sick and couldn't make it to the pool. But they turned the room into their own photoshoot — she looked absolutely stunning in a sheer top, struck some gorgeous sensual poses, and the pictures turned out fantastic.

cards.title: The Salt Desert
cards.art_description: Do not alter any details in the original image. Only extend the background naturally to fill a 6:5 landscape aspect ratio (1800×1500px minimum), matching the existing style, lighting, and color palette seamlessly.
cards.flavor_text: They fought at sunset. Nature forgave at sunrise.
cards.scene_context: The White Rann of Kutch — an absolutely magical salt desert stretching for kilometers. They went for a sunset viewing and loved it, though a fight broke out that evening. Lucky enough to return in the morning, they sat together on the salt flats, spoke, bonded, and admired the crystals — turning conflict into connection.

cards.title: The Pool and the Ocean
cards.art_description: Do not alter any details in the original image. Only extend the background naturally to fill a 6:5 landscape aspect ratio (1800×1500px minimum), matching the existing style, lighting, and color palette seamlessly.
cards.flavor_text: They cleaned the pool. They dipped the phone. They tripped in the ocean.
cards.scene_context: A beautiful weekend at a beachfront private pool villa in South Goa — just the two of them. They hopped between the pool and the ocean, laughed endlessly, damaged a phone clicking pool pictures, and even cleaned the pool themselves. Before one session, they randomly took some photos that turned out to be among the most cherished pictures of them together.

cards.title: A Sky Full of Stars
cards.art_description: Do not alter any details in the original image. Only extend the background naturally to fill a 5:7 portrait aspect ratio (1500×2100px minimum), matching the existing style, lighting, and color palette seamlessly.
cards.flavor_text: Look at the stars. Look how they shine for you.
cards.scene_context: Their first concert together — Coldplay in Gujarat. They planned every detail meticulously: the perfect seats, how to wrap up food, all the logistics. And it all worked out beautifully. Surrounded by glowing lights, swaying to the music, they captured some of their happiest pictures in a night that felt like pure magic.

cards.title: Buri Buri Ass Dance
cards.art_description: Shinchan faces away from the viewer, pants down, performing his infamous buri buri butt-shaking dance in the middle of a crowded park. His bare bottom wiggles proudly. Misae charges toward him, face bright red with fury, arm outstretched. Hiroshi covers his face in shame on a bench behind them. Other families stare in disbelief. Cherry blossom trees, sunny day, pure shameless Shinchan energy.
cards.flavor_text: Buri buri~!
cards.scene_context: Shinchan's signature provocation: dropping his pants and shaking his butt at the worst possible moment. It's pure mischief — deliberate, shameless, and guaranteed to send Misae into a rage. The buri buri dance is the single most recognizable Shinchan moment worldwide.

cards.title: Action Kamen & Chocobi
cards.art_description: Shinchan stands heroically on the living room couch in a homemade Action Kamen cape and mask, striking a dramatic pose. One hand raised in a power fist, the other clutching a box of Chocobi snacks like a sacred weapon. Behind him, the TV plays an Action Kamen episode. The living room wall cracks with imagined impact lines and explosion effects bleeding from his fantasy into reality. Bold anime action lines radiating outward.
cards.flavor_text: In his mind, he already saved the world.
cards.scene_context: Shinchan's hero worship of Action Kamen is one of his defining traits, and Chocobi snacks are his fuel. In his imagination, the living room becomes a battlefield and he's the star. The fantasy-reality bleed is classic Shinchan — hilarious and endearing.

cards.title: Nanako-sensei Crush
cards.art_description: Shinchan leans against a fence outside the kindergarten, one hand behind his head, the other offering a picked flower to Nanako-sensei with hearts floating around his head. Nanako smiles nervously, sweatdrop on her forehead. Kazama, Nene, Bo-chan, and Masao watch from behind a bush, jaws dropped. Afternoon sun, kindergarten playground in the background.
cards.flavor_text: Nanako-sensei, you smell like sunshine today!
cards.scene_context: Shinchan's shameless crush on his beautiful college-student teacher Nanako-sensei is one of the show's longest-running gags. He flirts with the confidence of a grown man, she has no idea how to handle a five-year-old suitor, and everyone else can only watch in stunned silence.

cards.title: Misae's Iron Fist
cards.art_description: Misae towers over Shinchan with her fist raised, a vein popping on her forehead. Shinchan stands below her with a comically enormous bump already growing on his head, shaped like a mountain, yet his expression is completely unfazed — he's already planning his next move. The living room behind them shows evidence of whatever chaos triggered this: knocked-over vase, crayon drawings on the wall, Shiro hiding behind the couch.
cards.flavor_text: The bump grew. So did his next idea.
cards.scene_context: The Misae head-bump is as iconic as Shinchan himself. No matter how many times she whacks him, the bump grows cartoonishly large and Shinchan is completely unfazed within seconds. It's the eternal cycle: mischief, punishment, repeat. Underneath it all, pure mother-son love.

cards.title: Kasukabe Defense Force
cards.art_description: The five members of the Kasukabe Defense Force march in a line through the neighborhood on a 'secret mission.' Shinchan leads with a stick as a sword. Kazama follows reluctantly with a clipboard. Nene carries her stuffed rabbit aggressively. Bo-chan floats along silently. Masao trails behind, already crying. Suburban Kasukabe streets, afternoon shadows, the group casting long heroic silhouettes despite being five-year-olds.
cards.flavor_text: Five kids. One plan. Zero chance it works.
cards.scene_context: The Kasukabe Defense Force — Shinchan, Kazama, Nene, Bo-chan, and Masao — is the greatest kindergarten squad in anime. Their 'missions' always go sideways, but the friendship is unbreakable. Each kid is a perfect archetype: the leader, the brain, the muscle, the mystery, and the crybaby.

cards.title: Himawari Chaos
cards.art_description: Baby Himawari sits in Shinchan's lap on the living room floor, both hands clamped onto his cheeks, stretching his face into a grotesque rubber mask. Shinchan's eyes bulge, mouth distorted, arms flailing. Himawari giggles with pure baby delight, completely in control. Misae watches from the kitchen doorway, torn between intervening and laughing. Toys scattered around them, afternoon light through the window. Warm, chaotic, and deeply funny.
cards.flavor_text: She's a baby. He never stood a chance.
cards.scene_context: Himawari may be a baby, but she rules the Nohara household. Her iron grip on Shinchan's face is one of their most iconic sibling moments — he screams, she laughs, and for once in his life Shinchan has met someone he can't out-chaos. The roles reverse completely.

cards.title: Shiro, Go Shopping!
cards.art_description: Shiro trots proudly down a quiet Kasukabe neighborhood street, a shopping bag hanging from his mouth. The bag is perfectly balanced, contents intact. He passes a row of houses, tail wagging slightly. Behind him in the distance, the Nohara house is visible with Misae waving from the doorway. Cherry blossom petals drift across the street. Other neighborhood dogs watch from fences, impressed. Late afternoon golden light, long shadows. The most competent member of the Nohara family on his daily mission.
cards.flavor_text: Shiro came back with everything. Correct change included.
cards.scene_context: When the Nohara family needs something from the store, they send Shiro. And Shiro delivers — every time, correct items, correct change. He's quietly the most reliable and competent member of the entire household. A loyal, unflappable dog navigating suburban Japan with more composure than any human in the show.

cards.title: The Wand Chooses the Wizard
cards.art_description: Young Harry Potter standing in Ollivanders wand shop. He holds a wand, and a sudden, brilliant warm glow illuminates his face. Dust motes dance in the magical breeze. Mr. Ollivander watches with wide, knowing eyes.
cards.flavor_text: "It is curious that you should be destined for this wand when its brother gave you that scar."
cards.scene_context: The defining moment Harry truly realizes he is a wizard. The connection between his wand and Voldemort's establishes the inescapable destiny that will tie them together until the very end.

cards.title: The Sorting Hat
cards.art_description: A small Harry Potter sits on the stool in the Great Hall, the enormous Sorting Hat draped over his eyes. Hundreds of candles float overhead. The four house tables stretch into the distance. The Hat's brim moves as it deliberates. Warm candlelight, gothic grandeur.
cards.flavor_text: Not Slytherin... not Slytherin...
cards.scene_context: Harry's first moments at Hogwarts. The Sorting Hat deliberates between Slytherin and Gryffindor while Harry desperately whispers his plea. This single choice shapes everything that follows — and it's Harry's choice, not the Hat's.

cards.title: Expecto Patronum!
cards.art_description: Harry standing on the edge of the Black Lake, wand raised high. A massive, brilliant silver Stag Patronus bursts from his wand, charging across the water to scatter a horde of descending Dementors. Pure, blinding magical light in the darkness.
cards.flavor_text: "I knew I could do it this time, because I'd already done it."
cards.scene_context: Prisoner of Azkaban. Harry realizes that no one is coming to save him—he has to save himself. Conjuring the corporeal Patronus is his transition from a boy relying on protectors to a powerful wizard in his own right.

cards.title: Priori Incantatem
cards.art_description: Harry and Voldemort locked in a desperate wand duel in the Little Hangleton graveyard. A blazing golden thread connects their wands. Beads of light travel along the thread. Ghostly echoes of Voldemort's victims surround them in the dark cemetery.
cards.flavor_text: "He will not be killed! He is mine!"
cards.scene_context: Goblet of Fire. Harry's first true duel with the resurrected Dark Lord. Their twin cores lock, saving Harry's life and allowing him to see the echoes of his parents one last time before escaping.

cards.title: The Ministry Duel
cards.art_description: Dumbledore and Voldemort dueling in the glittering Atrium of the Ministry of Magic. Voldemort conjures a massive serpent of dark fire; Dumbledore calmly traps it in a sphere of animated water. Shattered glass hangs suspended in the air.
cards.flavor_text: "It was foolish to come here tonight, Tom."
cards.scene_context: Order of the Phoenix. The only wizard Voldemort ever feared displays exactly why. A masterclass in magical combat that proves sheer power is nothing without absolute control and wisdom.

cards.title: After All This Time
cards.art_description: Snape's memories swirl in the Pensieve. Young Snape and young Lily stand in a sunlit meadow, her red hair catching the wind. The scene dissolves at the edges into silver memory-mist. Dumbledore's office surrounds the Pensieve, dark and somber. A single silver doe patronus emerges from the mist. Achingly beautiful, bittersweet light.
cards.flavor_text: Always.
cards.scene_context: Snape's memories in the Pensieve reveal the truth: his entire life was shaped by his love for Lily Potter. The silver doe patronus — matching hers — proves that Severus Snape protected Harry for decades out of undying love.

cards.title: The Mirror of Erised
cards.art_description: A young Harry Potter sitting on the cold stone floor at night, staring longingly into a massive ornate mirror. In the reflection, his parents, Lily and James, stand right behind him, smiling lovingly. His hand reaches out to touch the glass.
cards.flavor_text: It showed him everything he ever wanted — and could never have.
cards.scene_context: Harry's tragedy isn't that he must fight Voldemort, but that he grew up without the love of his family. The Mirror of Erised beautifully and heartbreakingly encapsulates the core motivation for everything he does.

cards.title: Crash into Radiator Springs
cards.art_description: Lightning McQueen tangled in power lines and road, having torn up the main street of a sleepy Route 66 town. Dust cloud settles. Broken asphalt, knocked-over traffic cones. The neon signs of Radiator Springs flicker in the desert dusk. Mater watches from behind a fence.
cards.flavor_text: Precision instrument of speed and aerodynamics. Stuck in a fence.
cards.scene_context: Lightning McQueen, the arrogant rookie racer, accidentally destroys the main street of a forgotten Route 66 town. This humbling crash forces him to slow down and discover what actually matters — the beginning of his entire character arc.

cards.title: Tractor Tipping
cards.art_description: Mater and McQueen hide behind a hay bale at the edge of a moonlit field, stifling laughter. A row of sleeping tractors snores peacefully. One tractor mid-tip, legs in the air, startled awake. In the far background, Frank the combine harvester's headlights flick on ominously. Dusty farmland, fireflies, mischievous night energy.
cards.flavor_text: I swear, Mater, if we die doing this—
cards.scene_context: Mater introduces McQueen to his favorite pastime: sneaking into fields at night and scaring sleeping tractors. It's juvenile, dangerous, and the exact moment McQueen starts having genuine fun for the first time — not racing-podium fun.

cards.title: Cruisin' Radiator Springs
cards.art_description: The newly paved main street of Radiator Springs at night. The vintage neon signs of Flo's V8 Cafe, Ramone's House of Body Art, and the Cozy Cone Motel are all glowing brilliantly in vibrant colors. The townsfolk cars cruise slowly down the street together, reflecting the neon light off their polished paint jobs. Beautiful, nostalgic atmosphere.
cards.flavor_text: "They're not just fixing the road. They're fixing the town."
cards.scene_context: After McQueen finishes fixing the road and gets a makeover, evening falls and the forgotten town's neon signs buzz back to life. It's the visual and emotional climax of McQueen bringing life back to Radiator Springs.

cards.title: Turn Right to Go Left
cards.art_description: Doc Hudson tearing around a dusty dirt track curve, executing a flawless, sweeping power-drift. A massive dust cloud kicks up behind his rear tires. Lightning McQueen watches in complete shock from behind a cluster of cacti in the foreground. Golden hour desert lighting, motion blur conveying intense speed and precision.
cards.flavor_text: "I'll tell you what. You drive like you fix roads. Lousy!"
cards.scene_context: Doc Hudson proves he isn't just a grumpy old judge—he still has the magic. The drifting technique he demonstrates here is exactly what saves McQueen from wiping out in the final Piston Cup race.

cards.title: Sally's Drive
cards.art_description: Sally and McQueen cruise side by side along a winding mountain road at golden hour. Below them, the vast Arizona desert stretches to the horizon. The old road hugs red rock cliffs, wildflowers line the shoulder. Sally glances at McQueen — he's not looking at the view anymore, he's looking at her. Warm cinematic light, sweeping landscape.
cards.flavor_text: Some roads change where you're going. Others change who you are.
cards.scene_context: Sally takes McQueen on a drive along the old mountain road, showing him the beauty of Route 66 before the interstate bypassed it. The moment McQueen stops looking at the view and starts looking at her marks his transformation from racer to person.

cards.title: Guido's Pit Stop
cards.art_description: Tiny blue Guido moving in an absolute blur around Lightning McQueen in the pit lane. Four tires are suspended in mid-air simultaneously as Guido wields his air wrench. In the neighboring pit box, Chick Hicks' massive, heavily equipped pit crew drop their grills and mustaches to the ground in utter shock. Fast-paced, comedic action.
cards.flavor_text: "Peet stop."
cards.scene_context: During the final Piston Cup tiebreaker race, Chick Hicks' massive pit crew mocks tiny Guido. In response, Guido single-handedly changes all four of McQueen's tires in under four seconds—the ultimate underdog triumph.

cards.title: Pushing The King Home
cards.art_description: McQueen stops inches from the finish line and reverses to push the battered, crumpled King across instead. The massive Dinoco 400 stadium is silent, then erupts. Chick Hicks crosses first but no one watches him. Sunset floods the track. An act of pure sportsmanship. Epic wide shot, crowd on their feet.
cards.flavor_text: A grumpy old race car once told him: it's just an empty cup.
cards.scene_context: McQueen stops inches from the finish line to push the wrecked King across instead, sacrificing the Piston Cup. The stadium erupts. He loses the race but wins something far more important — becoming the kind of racer Doc Hudson was.

cards.title: Pikachu Refuses the Pokeball
cards.art_description: Ash holds out a Pokeball on a dirt road outside Pallet Town. Pikachu sits defiantly, turning away with folded arms. Professor Oak's lab visible in the background. Morning light, the beginning of everything. Pikachu's cheeks spark slightly with annoyance.
cards.flavor_text: He didn't want a master. He wanted a partner.
cards.scene_context: The very first episode. Ash receives Pikachu as his starter Pokemon, but Pikachu refuses to enter the Pokeball. This defiant act establishes the entire series: their bond must be earned, not forced. It's where everything begins.

cards.title: Shielding Pikachu
cards.art_description: A battered Ash stands firm in front of an injured Pikachu, his arms spread wide to shield him from a massive flock of diving Spearows. Heavy rain pours down. A bright lightning strike begins to form above them.
cards.flavor_text: It took a storm to show what kind of Trainer he truly was.
cards.scene_context: Episode 1. Ash sacrifices himself to protect a Pokemon that has treated him with nothing but hostility. This single act of pure selflessness is what finally earns Pikachu's absolute, unwavering loyalty.

cards.title: Team Rocket Blasts Off
cards.art_description: Jessie, James, and Meowth launched into the sky by a Thunderbolt, their hot air balloon disintegrating around them. They spin into the distance as a star twinkles. Pikachu stands below, cheeks sparking, satisfied. Blue sky, contrail of smoke.
cards.flavor_text: Looks like Team Rocket's blasting off again!
cards.scene_context: The recurring gag that defines Pokemon's humor. Jessie, James, and Meowth's elaborate schemes always end the same way — blasted into the sky by Pikachu's Thunderbolt. Their persistence in failure is both hilarious and oddly admirable.

cards.title: Bye Bye Butterfree
cards.art_description: Ash, teary-eyed but smiling bravely, waves goodbye as his Butterfree flies away into the golden sunset alongside a pink Butterfree. Pikachu stands beside him, looking sad. The cliffs and ocean stretch into the distance.
cards.flavor_text: "You take care of her, Butterfree!"
cards.scene_context: The first time Ash releases a Pokemon. It taught an entire generation of kids a profound lesson about love and growing up: sometimes, loving someone means knowing when to let them go.

cards.title: Charizard's Seismic Toss
cards.art_description: Charizard silhouetted against the Earth from high up in the atmosphere, holding Magmar in a tight grip as they plummet downward in a spinning globe of fire. Intense kinetic energy and heat.
cards.flavor_text: He took the battle to the stratosphere, and brought the heat down with him.
cards.scene_context: The battle against Blaine. Charizard finally shows his true, terrifying power by carrying Magmar into the upper atmosphere and crashing down in an earth-shattering Seismic Toss. One of the most unforgettable anime battles.

cards.title: Tears of Stone
cards.art_description: Pikachu stands on the chest of Ash, turned to stone by crossfire between Mew and Mewtwo. Pikachu's cheeks spark desperately, sending Thunderbolts into Ash's stone form. Nothing works. Tears roll down Pikachu's face. All the Pokemon begin to cry, their tears glowing and floating upward. The stone begins to crack with golden light. Cathedral-like stadium space, deeply emotional.
cards.flavor_text: A desperate spark that faded into tears.
cards.scene_context: From Pokemon: The First Movie. Ash is turned to stone by crossfire between Mew and Mewtwo. Pikachu desperately shocks him, then cries. Every Pokemon's tears, carrying pure emotion, bring Ash back to life. The scene that made an entire generation cry in theaters.

cards.title: The World Champion
cards.art_description: Ash and Pikachu standing in the center of the massive Masters Eight stadium, holding the World Coronation Series trophy high. Confetti rains down. Both are exhausted but beaming with absolute joy.
cards.flavor_text: The boy from Pallet Town is finally the best.
cards.scene_context: After decades of journeys, leagues, and near-misses, Ash finally defeats Leon to become the strongest Trainer in the world. A moment that broke the internet and brought tears to fans worldwide.

cards.title: First Goomba Stomp
cards.art_description: Mario leaps high above a Goomba in World 1-1, one foot extended for the iconic stomp. Brick blocks and question mark blocks frame the scene. Green pipes, blue sky, pixel-perfect hills rendered in lush illustration style. Pure kinetic energy.
cards.flavor_text: One jump. One Goomba. An empire begins.
cards.scene_context: World 1-1 of Super Mario Bros. The first Goomba encounter teaches every player the fundamental mechanic: jump on enemies. This single stomp launched an entire genre and remains the most iconic moment in platforming history.

cards.title: The Underground Star
cards.art_description: Mario grabs a Super Star from a hidden block in the underground tunnels of World 1-2 and erupts in rainbow invincibility. His body flashes through every color as he sprints through a line of Goombas and Koopas in the dark brick corridors. Green pipes on either side, coins scattering, the low ceiling of the underground level stretching ahead. Trail of sparkles, speed lines, pure unstoppable euphoria.
cards.flavor_text: Ten seconds of being absolutely untouchable.
cards.scene_context: World 1-2 of Super Mario Bros. The underground level — dark brick tunnels, green pipes, and hidden blocks. Finding the Super Star here transforms Mario into a rainbow-flashing invincible force for ten glorious seconds. It's pure power fantasy, set to one of gaming's most recognizable jingles.

cards.title: The Inverted Pyramid
cards.art_description: Mario and Luigi ride dirt bikes side by side through the Tostarena desert, dust kicking up behind their wheels. Ahead of them, the massive Inverted Pyramid floats impossibly upside-down above the sand town, casting a geometric shadow across the dunes. Tostarena Town's Day of the Dead-inspired buildings visible below the pyramid. Bright desert sun, blue sky, the brothers grinning with adventure in their eyes.
cards.flavor_text: An upside-down pyramid was just another Tuesday for the Mario Brothers.
cards.scene_context: From The Super Mario Galaxy Movie. The opening scene after the prologue — Mario and Luigi ride dirt bikes through the Tostarena desert to investigate a disturbance at the Inverted Pyramid, a location from Super Mario Odyssey's Sand Kingdom. Inside the pyramid, they discover Yoshi hiding in a Warp Pipe. The moment that kicks off the movie's adventure.

cards.title: Jump Up, Super Star!
cards.art_description: Mario runs through a 2D throwback platforming section on the side of a neon-lit New Donk City skyscraper. Below him, the city festival is in full swing — Pauline sings on a rooftop stage, jazz band behind her, confetti in the air. The skyscraper's side is rendered as a classic 2D Mario level with girders and ladders, while the 3D city glows around it. Neon signs, spotlights, the skyline alive with celebration.
cards.flavor_text: A festival in the sky for the plumber who always saves the day.
cards.scene_context: From Super Mario Odyssey's Metro Kingdom. After saving New Donk City, Mario is honored at the city's festival. Pauline performs 'Jump Up, Super Star!' while Mario runs through a 2D throwback section on the side of a building — a love letter to Donkey Kong's original girders. Widely considered Odyssey's most joyful and celebrated moment.

cards.title: The Tiny Bros
cards.art_description: Baby Mario riding on Yoshi's head, tiny fists gripping his nose, face scrunched in a determined battle cry. Baby Luigi clinging to Yoshi's tail behind them, eyes squeezed shut, tears streaming comically. Yoshi mid-leap between mossy stone platforms, tongue out in concentration, completely unbothered despite carrying two screaming babies. A T-Rex snout pokes through jungle foliage far behind them but the babies are the focus. Waterfalls, butterflies, golden jungle light filtering through ferns.
cards.flavor_text: Two babies, one dinosaur, zero plan.
cards.scene_context: From The Super Mario Galaxy Movie. Bowser Jr. zaps Mario and Luigi with a Super Scope, shrinking them into helpless babies. Yoshi — who they'd only just befriended — scoops them up and races through Fossil Falls with a T-Rex in pursuit. Yoshi finds the Super Scope and restores them. The scene where Yoshi proves he's family.

cards.title: Sisters of Stardust
cards.art_description: Peach and Rosalina touch hands for the first time aboard the Comet Observatory. Cosmic energy erupts between their palms — stardust spirals outward, constellations ignite. Peach's eyes glow with newly awakened power. Rosalina, weary but relieved, smiles at her long-lost younger sister. Lumas swirl around them in awe. The observatory's dome opens to a vast field of stars. Celestial, luminous, overwhelming.
cards.flavor_text: Born from the same star. Found across a galaxy.
cards.scene_context: From The Super Mario Galaxy Movie. Peach discovers that Rosalina is her long-lost older sister — both born from stardust. When they touch hands, they can summon the power of the cosmos. Rosalina had sent infant Peach to the Mushroom Kingdom years ago to protect her. The emotional core of the film.

cards.title: Cosmic Rebirth
cards.art_description: Peach and Rosalina float above Planet Bowser, hands joined, cosmic energy cascading from their bodies. Below them, the planet transforms — lava cools into green fields, oceans form in craters, mountains rise from ash. Mario, Luigi, and Yoshi watch from a rocky outcrop as life blooms across the surface. Stardust rains down. The moment destruction becomes creation.
cards.flavor_text: From the ashes of a fallen world, a new universe has bloomed.
cards.scene_context: From The Super Mario Galaxy Movie's climax. After defeating the Bowsers, Peach revives a dying Rosalina. Together the sisters use their combined cosmic power to terraform Planet Bowser into a new living world. Destruction remade into creation — the ultimate expression of the sisters' bond.
